zh.lifeSummary: 當Lijsbeth van Mieren於1333年出生在荷蘭努-布拉班特時，她的父親Heer Jan Van Wijtfliet van Blaasveld en Cuijk21歲，母親Lijsbeth van Mieren19歲。她與Jan Arnoud Berthout Bac結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1391年5月13日之前。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lijsbeth van Mieren于1333年出生在荷兰努-布拉班特时，她的父亲Heer Jan Van Wijtfliet van Blaasveld en Cuijk21岁，母亲Lijsbeth van Mieren19岁。她与Jan Arnoud Berthout Bac结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1391年5月13日之前。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Mieren
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Konrad.
German and Dutch: patronymic from   Mies   2.

variants.name: Mies
meanings: South German: topographic name for someone who lived by a bog, Middle High German mies; or a habitational name from Mies, a place called with the same word.
Dutch and Flemish: from a short form of the personal name Bartholomeus (see Bartholomew ) or Michael .

en.lifeSummary: When Lijsbeth van Mieren was born in 1333, in North Brabant, Netherlands, her father, Heer Jan Van Wijtfliet van Blaasveld en Cuijk, was 21 and her mother, Lijsbeth van Mieren, was 19. She married Jan Arnoud Berthout Bac. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 2 daughters. She died before 13 May 1391.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lijsbeth van Mieren est née en 1333, à Brabant-septentrional, Pays-Bas, son père, Heer Jan Van Wijtfliet van Blaasveld en Cuijk, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Lijsbeth van Mieren, avait 19 ans. Elle a épousé Jan Arnoud Berthout Bac. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée avant 13 mai 1391.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lijsbeth van Mieren 1333 in Nord-Brabant, Niederlande geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Heer Jan Van Wijtfliet van Blaasveld en Cuijk 21 und ihre Mutter Lijsbeth van Mieren 19. Sie hat Jan Arnoud Berthout Bac geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb vor 13. Mai 1391.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lijsbeth van Mieren è nata il nel 1333 nel Brabante settentrionale, Paesi Bassi, sua padre, Heer Jan Van Wijtfliet van Blaasveld en Cuijk, aveva 21 anni e sua madre, Lijsbeth van Mieren, aveva 19 anni. Lei sposato Jan Arnoud Berthout Bac. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 2 figlie. Morì prima del 13 maggio 1391.

ja.lifeSummary: Lijsbeth van Mierenが1333年にオランダノオルド-ブラバントで生まれた時，父Heer Jan Van Wijtfliet van Blaasveld en Cuijkは21歳，母Lijsbeth van Mierenは19歳であった。Jan Arnoud Berthout Bacと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1391年5月13日以前に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lijsbeth van Mieren(이)가 1333년 네덜란드 노르드-브라반트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Heer Jan Van Wijtfliet van Blaasveld en Cuijk(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lijsbeth van Mieren(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 Jan Arnoud Berthout Bac(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1391년 5월 13일 이전 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lijsbeth van Mieren nasceu em 1333, em Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos, seu pai, Heer Jan Van Wijtfliet van Blaasveld en Cuijk, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Lijsbeth van Mieren, tinha 19 anos. Ela casou-se com Jan Arnoud Berthout Bac. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu antes 13 de maio de 1391.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lijsbeth van Mieren родилась в 1333 г. в местности нурд-брабант, Нидерланды, её отцу по имени Heer Jan Van Wijtfliet van Blaasveld en Cuijk было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Lijsbeth van Mieren, 19. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Jan Arnoud Berthout Bac. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла перед 13 май 1391 г.
